title: Ireland has recovered the remains of 99 infants from the former Tuam mother and baby home; DNA analysis may help restore some of their identities
summary_full: Ireland has recovered ninety-nine infant remains from a former mother and baby home.  Forensic excavations are ongoing at the Tuam site, revealing hidden burial chambers.  DNA analysis aims to identify the children and restore their identities to families.  This effort acknowledges a dark chapter in the country's institutional history.  The state is now confronting its past and treating the deceased with dignity.
